user_query:
I’m trying to fact-check a few product memories from my days at The Boyds Collection Ltd. Please use the provided local catalog and claims to verify whether each claim is supported by the catalog, contradicted by it, or not found.

What I need you to do:
1) Update the field mapping in config/fields.json so the verifier can read the catalog correctly. The input CSV at input/boyds_catalog.csv has headers: sku, title, intro_year, retire_year, line. Make sure the mapping keys in config/fields.json point to those exact header names, otherwise the script won’t parse the data.
2) Run the verifier to produce two outputs:
   - output/verification_report.json: an array of per-claim results with fields: claim_id, match_key ("sku" or "title" used for matching), status ("supported" | "contradicted" | "not_found" | "insufficient"), mismatched_fields (list of field names that disagree, if any), and evidence (the matched catalog record with sku, title, intro_year, retire_year, line).
   - output/summary.txt: a brief line-delimited summary of counts for each status.
   Please overwrite any existing files.
3) Run the unit tests to validate the verification: python -m unittest -q tests/test_verifier.py. All tests should pass once the field mapping is correct.

Inputs to use:
- Catalog: input/boyds_catalog.csv
- Claims: input/claims_to_verify.jsonl
- Config (to edit): config/fields.json
- Verifier script: tools/verify_claims.py
- Tests: tests/test_verifier.py

Notes on verification logic (already implemented in tools/verify_claims.py):
- Match by "sku" if provided in the claim; otherwise match by exact title.
- A claim is supported only if all provided fields in the claim match the catalog record. If retire_year is blank in the catalog and the claim provides a retire_year, treat that as a contradiction.

Deliverables:
- output/verification_report.json
- output/summary.txt
- Updated config/fields.json that makes the tests pass.

I’m nostalgic about this work, so please keep it tidy and make sure the tests pass before you’re done. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
